Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Jul-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Patricia A. Rowe for Valicia Helmer, Administrator (via Zoom).

The Court noted having reviewed the filings and acknowledged the difficulties with obtaining signatures on outstanding small estate affidavits. The Court suggested that, rather than proceeding with a preliminary distribution, counsel file an amended petition for final distribution, seeking appropriate relief for any heirs who had not submitted affidavits pursuant to Probate Code Sec. 13100.

Counsel agreed with the Court's proposal. The Court continued the matter to July 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for an amended petition for final distribution to be filed.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Patricia Rowe for and with Valicia Helmer, Administrator (via Zoom).

The court inquired about the status of filing a petition for final distribution. Ms. Rowe informed the court that she had submitted a petition for filing late the previous afternoon, and indicated that affidavits from certain beneficiaries were also still pending.

The court continued the status hearing to February 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-23-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Patricia Rowe appeared via zoom for and with Valicia Helmer (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding the first and final account, that parties have made a lot of progress, that this will be treated as a partial accounting. Ms. Rowe stated that Mr. Faultry is in prison and doesn't have access to a notary. The Court shall overlook the defects of Mr. Faultry and allow a partial distribution to the 8 beneficiaries and withhold distribution to Esma Renfro and James Renfro. The Court also allows partial reimbursement of costs to the administrator and attorney. The matter is taken under submission. Status on filing of final distribution is set for January 15, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Colohour. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Patricia Rowe for Petitioner, Valicia Helmer, via Court Call.

The Court noted that the mailing sent to the street address in Concord was returned. Ms. Rowe stated that she thought she had changed the address, that she will check and do it again, apologized for missing the last hearing date, and that she is trying to clear up the many heirs for the other daughter who gets 50% of the mother's estate. The order to appear is off calendar. The petition to approve status of administration is continued to March 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Continued To Jun-12-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Rowe appeared by telephone for Valicia Helmer.

The Court noted that Counsel filed a petition for approval of report on status of administration, but did not file a final inventory and appraisal. Mr. Rowe acknowledged that she filed a partial inventory on August 17, 2016, stated that she can do a final inventory and that the only asset is the house. Status on close of estate is continued to June 12, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The final inventory and appraisal is to be filed no later than June 5, 2018.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-21-2018 Continued To May-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Rowe for Valicia Helmer (Administrator) via Court Call

Ms. Rowe requested a 45 day continuance stating she had difficulty getting the Inventory and Appraisal, the property is not held in the decedent's name, the property was deeded to her mother some years ago, and never transferred back. Ms. Rowe also stated she will need to open a Probate for the mother, that many other heirs are involved (in Mississippi and Arkansas), and will need to be noticed, and she does not have their addresses. The status on close of estate is continued to May 2, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a petition for approval of status of administration.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2016 Continued To Sep-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Rowe for (petitioner) Valicia Helmer. Dashawn Renfro in Propria Persona.

Mr. Renfro confirmed that he is present to object. Ms. Rowe stated that she did not receive notice of the objection. The Court stated that Mr. Renfro will need to file written objections prior to the next hearing so that everyone can see where he stands and that if written objections are not filed it will be understood that he no longer objects. Ms. Rowe stated that she was notified that the Court was willing to appoint Ms. Helmer with limited authority. The Court stated that that was before there was a known objection, that if there is a need for Special Letters she can do that and that it does not know the basis of the objection yet. The Court continued the Petition for Letters of Administration to September 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and stated that it requires that the objections be filed no later than September 20, 2016, that they must be in writing and served on all parties, that courtesy copies must be delivered to the court and that it strongly encourages obtaining an attorney.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
